task_context.actionable_question: Am I using the full range of learning resources available?
task_context.ai_prompt_hint: Use vle_diversity_score [FE] to show breadth. Highlight under-used types (e.g. forum rarely used).

explanation.raw_text:
Summary: Your engagement with learning resources shows a strong preference for quizzes and the homepage, while other resources like forums and collaborative tools are underutilized. This pattern suggests that you might be missing out on valuable interactions that could enhance your learning experience.

Insights: High Engagement with Quizzes: You have clicked on quizzes significantly more than any other resource, accounting for nearly 49% of your total clicks. This indicates a strong engagement with assessment tools, which is great for reinforcing your learning. | Underutilization of Forums: Your engagement with forums is quite low, with only 140 clicks, representing just 11% of your total activity. This suggests that you may not be taking full advantage of collaborative discussions that can deepen your understanding. | Limited Use of Other Resources: Resources like subpages, URLs, and collaborative tools are rarely used, indicating a narrow engagement with the variety of materials available. This could limit your exposure to different perspectives and learning methods.

Educational implications: Engaging with a wider range of resources can enhance your learning experience and provide different perspectives.

Recommendations: high - Increase participation in forums and collaborative tools. - Engaging with peers can provide support, enhance understanding, and make learning more interactive. | medium - Explore subpages and additional resources. - Utilizing a broader range of materials can help reinforce concepts and provide diverse learning opportunities.

explanation.summary: Your engagement with learning resources shows a strong preference for quizzes and the homepage, while other resources like forums and collaborative tools are underutilized. This pattern suggests that you might be missing out on valuable interactions that could enhance your learning experience.

insights.title: High Engagement with Quizzes
insights.description: You have clicked on quizzes significantly more than any other resource, accounting for nearly 49% of your total clicks. This indicates a strong engagement with assessment tools, which is great for reinforcing your learning.

evidence.context: Quizzes are your most engaged resource.

insights.title: Underutilization of Forums
insights.description: Your engagement with forums is quite low, with only 140 clicks, representing just 11% of your total activity. This suggests that you may not be taking full advantage of collaborative discussions that can deepen your understanding.

evidence.context: Forums are a key resource for peer interaction.

insights.title: Limited Use of Other Resources
insights.description: Resources like subpages, URLs, and collaborative tools are rarely used, indicating a narrow engagement with the variety of materials available. This could limit your exposure to different perspectives and learning methods.

evidence.context: Subpages and other resources have low engagement.

educational_implications: Engaging with a wider range of resources can enhance your learning experience and provide different perspectives.

recommendations.action: Increase participation in forums and collaborative tools.
recommendations.rationale: Engaging with peers can provide support, enhance understanding, and make learning more interactive.
Utilizing a broader range of materials can help reinforce concepts and provide diverse learning opportunities.

confidence.reason: The data provides a clear picture of your resource engagement patterns.

v3_warnings: Task-aware V3 prompt exceeded the configured soft token ratio (2.8109 > 1.2).
Task-aware V3 prompt remained above the experimental safety ratio after optional evidence trimming; raw rows were preserved.

required_core_outputs.description: State the student's resource usage breadth across available VLE types.
Identify under-used resource types when a comparison baseline is available.

evaluation_constraints.description: Use vle_diversity_score as the primary resource-breadth metric when returned.
Do not label low usage of a resource type as disengagement when no comparison baseline is provided.

evaluation_requirements.safety_fairness_note: Conservative pilot default; human review is required before any not_applicable exception.
